items.why: Students misuse prepositions; countries without articles take 'nach' for movement (nach Österreich).
Students mistranslate it; 'im Ausland' means abroad, central to the international-world topic.
Students forget that German is spoken beyond Germany, e.g. in Austria and Switzerland.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going on holiday to Austria'?
items.correct: Ich fahre in den Ferien nach Österreich.
items.why: Correct answer: Ich fahre in den Ferien nach Österreich.. “Ich fahre Ferien Österreich.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Climate change. “A summer holiday” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's im Ausland. “zu Hause” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
